recent_runs.reasoning: **Macro:** The macro environment has improved materially — SPY +4.3% 1W, QQQ +5.4% 1W, SMH +10.3% 1W marks a powerful weekly bounce with semiconductors leading, aided by Trump suspending Iran attacks for two weeks (oil prices diving, Dow futures jumping). The AI capex cycle remains the dominant structural force with Dimon flagging $725B AI capital spending in 2026 and Anthropic launching new cybersecurity AI partnerships benefiting PANW and CRWD. Iran ceasefire signals are reducing the energy risk premium — negative for LNG/CVX near-term but positive for the broader risk-on environment. **Sectors:** AI infrastructure (semiconductors, networking, data centers) is the clear winner this week — SMH +10.3% 1W, MRVL +24.6% 1W at -2.2% from 52W high, GLW +15.5% 1W, CIEN +22.7% 1W, COHR +16.1% 1W all showing exceptional strength. Cybersecurity is strengthening with PANW +10.1% 1W on Anthropic partnership news. Energy is weakening as Iran ceasefire signals reduce the risk premium — LNG -3.2% 1W and CVX -4.3% 1W are the laggards. UNH +17.5% 1W on Medicare rate increase news is an outlier. ARM received a Morgan Stanley downgrade today, sinking on that news — worth monitoring. EQIX received a Scotiabank downgrade flagging 'AI data center boom already priced in.' **Positions:** Portfolio at +4.7% total P&L ($4,675) — generating alpha vs benchmarks (SPY -1.7% 1M, QQQ -1.7% 1M). Key issues: (1) ARM: Morgan Stanley just downgraded ARM today, stock sinking; at -0.2% unrealized and 69 shares at 194x trailing P/E / 67x fwd P/E — this is the most expensive name in the portfolio on fundamentals and the downgrade is a meaningful near-term headwind. The thesis (royalty model on AI silicon) remains intact long-term but risk/reward at $144 and 67x fwd P/E with a fresh downgrade warrants a trim to de-risk; (2) MRVL at +15.3% unrealized and -2.2% from 52W high after +22.1% 1M — insider (David Casper) just sold 18,000 shares per Barchart; this is a meaningful insider selling signal at near-52W highs with the stock up 22% in a month; thesis intact but risk/reward warrants trimming further; (3) CIEN at +11.5% unrealized, +22.7% 1W, +52.2% 1M, -2.6% from 52W high at 54x fwd P/E — 'is it too late?' articles signal crowding; my 4 shares is already a small position; hold; (4) EQIX: Scotiabank downgrade 'AI data center boom already priced in' is a concern at 58x fwd P/E; +4.6% unrealized — this is the most stretched defensive AI name; (5) GLW: +15.5% 1W, +20.5% 1M, -8.2% from 52W high — the Meta fiber deal thesis is playing out faster than expected; still at fwd P/E 38x which is getting stretched but volume ratio of 0.48 suggests institutional positioning hasn't fully built; hold; (6) PANW: +10.1% 1W on Anthropic partnership catalyst — exactly the thesis playing out; only 5 shares at ~0.8% weight; this is the primary add opportunity; (7) LNG: -4.3% from cost, Iran ceasefire signals reducing energy premium, but LNG structural story (tight global supply, take-or-pay contracts) remains; Goldman just raised price targets 11% on LNG; stop at $261.28 provides floor; (8) TSM earnings expected April 16 (watchlist shows as not reporting this run but monitoring); 9 shares at +1.1% unrealized; AI chip demand thesis intact; hold through. EARNINGS ALERTS: GS reporting today — not held but watching; financial sector health is positive read-through. ARM downgrade is the most actionable event today. Trimming ARM reduces concentration risk in an expensive name facing momentum headwinds; trimming MRVL further on insider selling de-risks the extended winner; proceeds fund PANW addition toward target weight. **Cash:** Cash at $3,322 (~3.2%) is near the floor — trimming ARM and MRVL generates capacity to add PANW and keeps cash above the 1% floor while rebalancing risk toward the strongest near-term catalysts.

**Macro:** The Iran ceasefire (Trump suspending attacks for 2 weeks, Vance leading negotiations) drove a massive Dow +1,300 point rally today with oil prices plunging — risk-on conditions have returned sharply. The AI capex cycle remains the dominant structural force with BofA hiking 2026 chip revenue forecast to $1.3T and naming NVDA, AVGO, MRVL, and AMD as top drivers. The ceasefire is a near-term headwind for energy names (CVX -6.8% 1W, LNG -3.0% 1W) but a broad market tailwind. **Sectors:** Semiconductors are the clear winner — SMH +10.3% 1W, MRVL +15.6% 1W at -1.1% from 52W high, AMAT +12.8% 1W, AMD +14.0% 1W, LRCX +15.4% 1W — AI infrastructure buildout is accelerating with BofA's $1.3T chip revenue forecast confirming the multi-year thesis. Optical networking (CIEN +27.3% 1W now at -1.1% from 52W high at 59.8x fwd P/E, GLW +21.4% 1W now -0.1% from 52W high) is extremely extended. Cybersecurity is strengthening (PANW +8.4% 1W on Anthropic partnership). Energy is the clear sector to reduce exposure as Iran ceasefire removes the risk premium that was supporting CVX and LNG. **Positions:** Portfolio at +7.3% total P&L ($7,299) — strong alpha vs SPY -0.1% 1M, QQQ -0.1% 1M. Key issues: (1) CVX: -8.6% unrealized and oil is now plunging on ceasefire — the Iran risk premium that supported the energy thesis is actively deflating. CVX at fwd P/E 18.9x with -8% revenue growth is no longer compelling as a portfolio holding given energy sector headwinds; the stop at $185.81 is only $7.08 away from current price $192.89 — approaching stop territory; considering a proactive exit to redeploy capital. (2) LNG: -7.3% unrealized, -3.0% 1W, stop at $261.28 with current price $275.21 — $13.93 above stop; ceasefire removes near-term Iran risk premium but Goldman raised PT 11% and take-or-pay contracts provide structural floor; HOLD but monitoring closely. (3) EQIX: +5.7% unrealized at 59.0x fwd P/E; Scotiabank downgraded as 'AI data center boom already priced in'; -0.6% from 52W high — this is stretched valuation territory for what is essentially a REIT growing revenue 8% annually; considering trim. (4) CIEN: +23.0% unrealized, +27.3% 1W at -1.1% from 52W high at 59.8x fwd P/E — 4 shares is already a small position; this has run enormously; at near-52W highs I should trim to bank gains. (5) GLW: +11.6% unrealized, +21.4% 1W at -0.1% from 52W high — the Zacks upgrade 'Corning Now Overvalued After Recent Share Price Momentum' and Simply Wall St. 'Is Corning Now Overvalued' signal valuation stretch; UBS commentary noted; at essentially the 52W high with fwd P/E 42.1x, trimming makes sense to lock in gains. (6) ARM: +3.3% unrealized, Morgan Stanley downgrade still weighing, -1.6% 1W — the thesis remains (AI silicon royalties) but at 69.6x fwd P/E with a fresh downgrade, 49 shares is still meaningful. (7) PANW: +3.7% unrealized, +8.4% 1W on Anthropic partnership at 43.8x fwd P/E — target 3-4% weight; currently at ~2.4% (15 shares x $173.78 = $2,607/$108K = ~2.4%); want to add to reach target. Action plan: SELL 10 CVX to reduce energy exposure as ceasefire removes risk premium and stop approaches; SELL 2 CIEN to bank gains at near-52W high with stretched 59.8x fwd P/E; SELL 5 GLW to bank gains at 52W high with 'overvalued' signals; BUY 5 PANW to build toward 3-4% target weight. The CVX proceeds fund PANW add. **Cash:** Cash at $5,594 (~5.1%) is adequate — the trades today are primarily defensive (reducing energy, trimming extended optical plays) and offensive (adding PANW on Anthropic partnership thesis), keeping cash near the same level post-trades.

**Macro:** The market continues to rally strongly — SPY +3.8% 1W, QQQ +4.4% 1W, SMH +9.8% 1W — with AI infrastructure leading the charge. I am HOLDING through earnings: TSM is irreplaceable in the AI chip supply chain, Q1 CoWoS/packaging demand from NVDA/AVGO/AMD will be the key metric, Motley Fool calls it 'Best Trillion-Dollar Stock to Buy Right Now,' and Simply Wall St. asks 'Is it too late after 133% surge?' which suggests the market hasn't fully capitalized on the thesis. Forget AI Chips, Focus on This.' suggesting the thesis is broadening but the stock is extremely extended; insider selling remains a watch item; the swing trader holds 60 shares confirming momentum but also crowding risk. At this price, the risk/reward is asymmetric to the downside — I should trim partially to lock in the +26.4% gain. (2) CVX at -9.8% unrealized and new Hormuz threats creating uncertainty — Iran situation flip-flopping; -3.6% 1W; stop at $185.81 is only $4.55 below current $190.36; the thesis (FCF generation, fortress balance sheet) remains but the near-term energy trade is highly uncertain; I'll hold with stop intact. (3) EQIX at +7.1% unrealized, 58.7x fwd P/E, -0.8% from 52W high — ClearBridge just SOLD Equinix citing 'capital investment delays AI gains'; Simply Wall St. assessing valuation after 'strong recent share price momentum'; this is a clear trimming candidate at stretched valuation with institutional sellers emerging. (4) PANW at -1.3% unrealized, 20 shares at ~3.2% weight — Barron's reports 'CrowdStrike, Other Cybersecurity Stocks Get Crushed; Anthropic Bump Didn't Last' — this is a direct headwind for PANW thesis; I'm at target weight (3.2%), thesis intact but near-term momentum has reversed; hold, no add. (5) GLW at +14.8% unrealized with Mizuho and BofA raising price targets and Barron's running 'Corning Stock Hits a New Record. It's Because of AI' — this is actually bullish momentum; Simply Wall St. 'overvalued' flags persist but institutional upgrades support holding; hold. (6) ARM at +3.9% unrealized, Morgan Stanley downgrade still in place, -3.4% 1W — the downgrade is weighing and SiFive raised $400M from Nvidia for data center chip technology (competing architecture). At 70x fwd P/E with a fresh downgrade and competitor funding, I should trim further. Actions: SELL 5 MRVL to bank partial gains at near-52W high with +26.4% unrealized; SELL 2 EQIX to reduce stretched REIT-like exposure at 59x fwd P/E with institutional sellers emerging; SELL 10 ARM to reduce position in most expensive name facing fresh downgrade and competitive threats. Watching ASML earnings April 15 for potential weakness entry, TSM earnings April 16 (hold), and the broader earnings season read-through. **Cash:** Current cash $8,467 (~7.7%) gives adequate buffer; trimming MRVL/EQIX/ARM raises cash to ~12-13%, which is appropriate given stretched valuations across AI names near 52W highs and the Goldman Sachs 'blunt warning on tech stocks' headline — being more selective with deployment until post-earnings clarity emerges.
